tools.name: batch_read
tools.title: Batch Read Local Files
tools.description: Read a bounded batch of approved project-relative text files. Use this for known file sets or small globs instead of many file_read_path calls. Keep maxFiles and maxTotalBytes modest for hosted ChatGPT safety checks.

files.description: Project-relative file paths to read. Do not pass absolute paths.

globs.description: Optional rg-style glob patterns relative to projectPath, for example src/**/*.java.

tools.name: bridge_health
tools.title: Bridge Health
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for bridge.health. Check local and optional public bridge health endpoints.

tools.name: bridge.activity
tools.title: Bridge Activity
tools.description: Read recent local bridge tool-call records and audit events. Use this to inspect what ChatGPT actually asked the MCP server to do.

tools.name: bridge.health
tools.title: Bridge Health
tools.description: Check local and optional public bridge health endpoints.

tools.name: bridge.logs
tools.title: Bridge Logs
tools.description: Read recent bridge and tunnel log lines from the configured log directory.

tools.name: bridge.status
tools.title: Bridge Status
tools.description: Return local bridge and tunnel launchd service status.

tools.name: cloud.download
tools.title: Download Cloud File
tools.description: Download a ChatGPT/App-provided HTTPS file URL into an approved local workspace. Use this when a cloud-side file has a download link and should be synced to local disk.

tools.name: code.read
tools.title: Read Files
tools.description: Read one or more text files from a local project. If the user provides absolute local paths like /home/... or /Users/..., prefer file.read_path or file_read_path instead.

tools.name: code.read_range
tools.title: Read File Range
tools.description: Read a bounded line range from one text file inside a project.

tools.name: code.search
tools.title: Search Code
tools.description: Search local project files with ripgrep-compatible output.

tools.name: codex_result
tools.title: Codex Runner Result
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for codex.result. Return a compact task summary by default; set includeLog/includeDiff/includeHandoff only when needed.

tools.name: codex_status
tools.title: Codex Runner Status
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for codex.status. Read one Codex Runner task or list recent task records.

tools.name: codex_task_start
tools.title: Start Codex Runner Task
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for codex.task_start. Start a local Codex Runner job from a handoffId or scoped project task. Prefer this over shell tools.

tools.name: codex.cancel
tools.title: Cancel Codex Runner Task
tools.description: Cancel a running Codex Runner task by task id.

tools.name: codex.result
tools.title: Codex Runner Result
tools.description: Return a compact Codex Runner result summary. Full log, diff, and handoff metadata are opt-in to reduce hosted ChatGPT safety-check blocks.

tools.name: codex.status
tools.title: Codex Runner Status
tools.description: Read one Codex Runner task or list recent task records for the native app queue.

tools.name: codex.task_start
tools.title: Start Codex Runner Task
tools.description: High-level ChatGPT entrypoint for local Codex work. Creates a persisted task record that the native app can show in Codex Runner; prefer this over raw shell.exec for broad local work.

tools.name: file_list
tools.title: List Directory
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for file.list. List files and directories inside an approved local workspace.

tools.name: file_read_path
tools.title: Read Absolute Local Paths
tools.description: Compatibility alias for file.read_path. Read one or more absolute text file paths from approved local/Linux/Mac workspace roots. Use this when ChatGPT asks for file_read_path or when dotted MCP tool names are not available in the current client.

paths.description: Absolute local file paths to read. Each path must be inside bridge.policy allowedProjectRoots.

tools.name: file_write
tools.title: Write File
tools.description: Compatibility alias for file.write. Create or overwrite a text file inside a project.

tools.name: file.copy
tools.title: Copy File
tools.description: Copy a file inside a project.

tools.name: file.delete
tools.title: Delete File
tools.description: Delete a file inside a project.

tools.name: file.list
tools.title: List Directory
tools.description: List files and directories inside a project directory without reading file contents.

tools.name: file.mkdir
tools.title: Create Directory
tools.description: Create a directory inside a project.

tools.name: file.move
tools.title: Move File
tools.description: Move or rename a file or directory inside a project.

tools.name: file.patch
tools.title: Patch File
tools.description: Replace exact text inside a file. Fails unless the old text is found exactly once, unless replaceAll is true.

tools.name: file.read_path
tools.title: Read Absolute Local Paths
tools.description: Read one or more absolute text file paths from approved local/Linux/Mac workspace roots. Use this whenever the user gives a full local path such as /home/user/project/file.py or /Users/alex/project/file.md; do not use cloud Python or a sandbox shell to check these local paths.

paths.description: Absolute local file paths to read. Each path must be inside bridge.policy allowedProjectRoots.

tools.name: file.stat
tools.title: File Stat
tools.description: Return metadata for a file or directory inside a project, optionally including sha256 for files.

tools.name: file.write
tools.title: Write File
tools.description: Create or overwrite a text file inside a project. Use code.read first when modifying an existing file.

tools.name: git.checkpoint
tools.title: Git Checkpoint
tools.description: Return the current HEAD commit. Use this before a risky edit so git.revert can restore the working tree to this commit.

tools.name: git.diff
tools.title: Git Diff
tools.description: Return git diff summary and structured diff for the current working tree.

ref.description: Optional git ref to diff against. Defaults to HEAD.

tools.name: git.revert
tools.title: Git Revert Working Tree
tools.description: Destructive: restore tracked files to a checkpoint and optionally remove untracked files.

tools.name: git.status
tools.title: Git Status
tools.description: Return git branch, HEAD, and porcelain status for a project.

tools.name: handoff_create
tools.title: Create Codex Handoff
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for handoff.create. Create a structured handoff package for local Codex Runner. This does not run code; call codex_task_start with the returned handoffId.

tools.name: handoff.create
tools.title: Create Codex Handoff
tools.description: Validate and persist a structured handoff package for local Codex Runner. This does not execute code; call codex.task_start with the returned handoffId to run it.

tools.name: local_bundle_dir
tools.title: Bundle Local Directory
tools.description: Bundle an approved local directory summary plus selected text files in one call. Use this when the user wants multiple local files or a compact project snapshot.

files.description: Optional relative files to include with content.

tools.name: local_list_dir
tools.title: List Local Directory
tools.description: Use this first when the user asks whether ChatGPT can see a local path. It lists an approved local directory only; it does not read file contents.

projectPath.description: Absolute approved local directory path, such as /path/to/project or /Users/name/project.

dir.description: Subdirectory inside projectPath. Use "." for the directory itself.

tools.name: local_read_file
tools.title: Read Local File
tools.description: Read one approved local text file. Use this only when the user asks to read a specific file, not when they ask to inspect or list a directory.

file.description: Relative file path inside projectPath. Do not pass a directory.

tools.name: local_workspace_action
tools.title: Local Workspace Action
tools.description: Preferred single entrypoint for ChatGPT. Choose action=list_dir for directories, read_file for one file, write_file to save one text file, or bundle_dir for a directory summary plus selected files.

path.description: Relative directory or file path inside projectPath.

files.description: Only used by bundle_dir. Optional relative files to include with content.

content.description: Only used by write_file. Full text content to write.

createDirs.description: Only used by write_file. Create parent directories when missing.

tools.name: local_write_file
tools.title: Write Local File
tools.description: Create or overwrite one approved local text file. Use this when the user explicitly asks to save, create, or update a file in an approved local workspace.

file.description: Relative file path inside projectPath. Do not pass an absolute path.

tools.name: policy_read
tools.title: Read Bridge Policy
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible alias for policy.read. Read approved roots, skill roots, deny globs, and shell rules.

tools.name: policy.read
tools.title: Read Bridge Policy
tools.description: Read the active local safety policy: approved workspace roots, skill roots, denied file globs, and shell rules.

tools.name: policy.validate
tools.title: Validate Bridge Policy
tools.description: Validate a proposed policy before saving it in the native app or bridge.policy.json. This tool does not write files.

tools.name: port.check
tools.title: Check Port
tools.description: Check whether a local TCP port is listening and identify the owning process when possible.

tools.name: process.list
tools.title: List Project Processes
tools.description: List processes started by this bridge runtime.

tools.name: process.start
tools.title: Start Project Process
tools.description: Start a long-running project process and persist its pid/log path.

tools.name: process.stop
tools.title: Stop Project Process
tools.description: Stop a process previously started by process.start. Requires confirm=true.

tools.name: project.bundle
tools.title: Bundle Project Context
tools.description: Read a local directory summary, selected text files, and optional git diff in one call. Use this when the user wants several local files inspected or copied into a cloud-side downloadable artifact: read local first with this tool, then generate the downloadable copy from the returned content.

tools.name: project.index
tools.title: Project Index
tools.description: Return a higher-level project index: package scripts, key files, detected tests, and top-level structure.

tools.name: project.scripts
tools.title: Project Package Scripts
tools.description: Return scripts from package.json for a Node project.

tools.name: project.snapshot
tools.title: Project Snapshot
tools.description: Get local project context: git state, language, package manager, and a bounded file tree. Use this before editing a project.

path.description: Absolute path to the project directory

tools.name: service.restart
tools.title: Restart Bridge Service
tools.description: Restart one fixed launchd service: bridge or ngrok. Requires confirm=true.

tools.name: shell_exec
tools.title: Shell Exec
tools.description: ChatGPT-compatible shell alias. Run a shell command inside an approved projectPath and return stdout/stderr. Uses bridge policy denyPatterns, timeout, and output limits.

projectPath.description: Absolute approved workspace root used as the command cwd.

command.description: Shell command to run inside projectPath.

tools.name: shell.exec
tools.title: Run Shell Command
tools.description: Run a shell command in the project directory and return stdout/stderr. Use for tests, builds, and local inspection.

tools.name: skill.bundle
tools.title: Bundle Local Skill
tools.description: Bundle a local SKILL.md with directly referenced local text files such as references/*.md. Use this when a task needs the whole local skill context.

activationId.description: Required to include referenced files. Use the activationId returned by skill.read.

tools.name: skill.list
tools.title: List Local Skills
tools.description: List SKILL.md files from approved skill roots. Defaults to ~/.codex/skills when available.

tools.name: skill.read
tools.title: Read Local Skill
tools.description: Read one approved local SKILL.md file. Use this before following a local Codex skill from ChatGPT.

skill.description: Skill id, name, relative directory, or relative path to SKILL.md

tools.name: skill.route
tools.title: Route Task To Local Skills
tools.description: Recommend local skills for a task and return a short follow-up prompt for reading or bundling them.

tools.name: skill.search
tools.title: Search Local Skills
tools.description: Search approved local SKILL.md files by name, description, path, and content preview.

tools.name: task.finish
tools.title: Finish Task
tools.description: Mark a persisted task/session as done or blocked.

tools.name: task.note
tools.title: Add Task Note
tools.description: Append a note to a persisted task/session.

tools.name: task.start
tools.title: Start Task
tools.description: Start a lightweight persisted task/session record for multi-step work.

tools.name: task.status
tools.title: Task Status
tools.description: Read one task or list recent task/session records.

tools.name: test.detect
tools.title: Detect Test Commands
tools.description: Detect likely project test commands without running them.

tools.name: test.run
tools.title: Run Tests
tools.description: Run a detected or explicit test command in a project directory.

tools.name: trace.session_current
tools.title: Get Current Trace Session
tools.description: Read the current active trace session used by subsequent tool calls.

tools.name: trace.session_end
tools.title: End Trace Session
tools.description: End the currently active trace session. Provide sessionId to end a specific session.

tools.name: trace.session_start
tools.title: Start Trace Session
tools.description: Create a logical trace session id for grouping subsequent tool calls by conversation/task/project.

tools.name: workspace.add
tools.title: Add Workspace
tools.description: Register a local project path with a short workspace name.

tools.name: workspace.list
tools.title: List Workspaces
tools.description: List registered local workspaces.

tools.name: workspace.resolve
tools.title: Resolve Workspace
tools.description: Resolve a workspace name to its project path. If name is omitted, resolves the default workspace.
